question: In the production of what minerals does Great Britain exceed every other country?
answer: Coal, iron, salt, lead, and tin.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Name the largest seven rivers in the United States.
answer: Describe them. Mississippi, Missouri, Arkansas, Rio Grande, Columbia, Nebraska, and Red.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What are the principal uses of rivers?
answer: For drainage, commerce and milling.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What important places of the globe are situated on or near the 40th parallel north latitude?
answer: Philadelphia, Columbus, Indianapolis, Springfield, Denver, Pekin, Constantinople, and Madrid.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What part of the United States is in the same latitude north of the equator, that the southern part of Africa is south of it?
answer: The boundary line which divides the States of Mississippi, Alabama and Georgia from Tennessee.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What are the latitudes of Cape Horn and the Cape of Good Hope?
answer: Cape Horn is fifty-three degrees south, and Good Hope about thirty-two degrees.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What is the difference between the natural location of commercial and manufacturing cities?
answer: Commercial cities require good harbors and water communication to facilitate commerce, while manufacturing cities depend very much upon water power to run their machinery.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Why is the water of Great Salt Lake not fresh?
answer: Lakes of this class lose water by evaporation only, and retain the salt and other materials carried into them by their affluent rivers.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Name five of the principal articles exported by the people of the United States.
answer: Cotton, wheat, pork, cheese, machinery.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Name some of the principal articles imported?
answer: Wool, tea, coffee and spices, dry goods, sugar and liquors.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What are the great waterways for the internal commerce of the United States?
answer: The Atlantic system, the Mississippi system, the Great Lakes with the St. Lawrence River, and the canals.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Describe the Mississippi system.
answer: This system contains fifty-six great tributaries, containing 17,000 miles navigable to steamboats and more than 20,000 by barge. It carries an immense commerce, but in some localities the annual floods and droughts prevent a continuous shipment. Below the mouth of the Ohio the Mississippi sometimes rises more than fifty feet.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What does the St. Lawrence system comprise?
answer: The Great Lakes and the navigable streams which flow into and through the St. Lawrence River. This system is closed for several months in the year on account of ice, but floods or droughts never affect it.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Where are the chief canals?
answer: They lie at the entrance of Lake Superior, the Niagara Falls, the Rapids of the St. Lawrence and the Erie Canal.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What change in the political status of the peninsula of Korea occurred in 1910?
answer: It was annexed to the Japanese Empire.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: Give its area and population.
answer: It is about 600 miles long and 135 miles wide, and contains 10,000,000 inhabitants.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What country was absorbed by the British Empire in 1900, by war and conquest?
answer: The Transvaal Republic, in South Africa, a country about three times the size of Ohio; rich in gold and diamond mines, but only having about 1,500,000 population.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: In what parts of the world do they raise the most corn?
answer: The United States, south central Europe and southeastern part of South America.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: In what parts the most wheat?
answer: The United States, Russia, France, India and Austria-Hungary.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: In what parts do they produce the most sugar?
answer: Cuba first and Java second.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: In what parts the most coffee?
answer: Brazil, Venezuela, Guatemala, Dutch East Indies.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What country produces the most coal, iron, petroleum, wheat, cotton, and manufactured goods?
answer: The United States. No wonder we are a world power.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: The area and population of the five principal nations, together with their production of wheat, cotton, coal and manufactured goods.
answer: Area British Empire 11,444,000 Russian Empire 8,800,000 France & Poss. 4,300,000 United States & Poss. 3,800,000 Germany & Poss. 1,200,000 Population British Empire 392,800,000 Russian Empire 143,800,000 United States & Poss. 101,000,000 France & Poss. 91,000,000 Germany & Poss. 72,000,000 Wheat United States 737,000,000 Bushels Russia 711,000,000 Bushels France 365,000,000 Bushels India 300,000,000 Bushels Austria-Hungary 224,000,000 Bushels Cotton United States 10,600,000 Bales India 2,600,000 Bales China 1,200,000 Bales Egypt 1,150,000 Bales Korea 300,000 Bales Coal United States 414,000,000 Tons Great Britain 281,000,000 Tons Germany 222,000,000 Tons Austria-Hungary 45,000,000 Tons France 37,000,000 Tons Manufactured Goods United States 13,000 Million Dollars United Kingdom 5,000 Million Dollars Germany 4,500 Million Dollars France 3,500 Million Dollars
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON GEOGRAPHY

question: What is mathematics?
answer: The science of quantity.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is quantity?
answer: Anything that can be increased, diminished or measured.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define arithmetic as a science and as an art.
answer: As a science, it logically investigates and philosophically classifies and arranges the principles and rules of the subject; as an art, it applies these principles.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Upon what is arithmetic founded, and how are its operations carried on?
answer: It is founded on notation, and its operations are carried on by means of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is arithmetical language?
answer: The method of expressing numbers. It is of two kinds, oral and written.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a unit?
answer: One, or a single thing.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a number? The power of a number? A root?
answer: A number is a unit, or a collection of units. The power of a number is the product produced by repeating a number so many times as a factor. A root is a factor repeated to produce power.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an integer?
answer: A whole number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between an abstract number and a concrete number?
answer: An abstract number is one used without reference to any particular thing; as, 1, 6, 15, etc. A concrete number is one that has reference to a particular thing; as, one tree, five boys, fifteen houses, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a sign?
answer: A sign is a character indicating an operation to be performed; as, ÷, X, —, +, √.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the sign of aggregation?
answer: The sign of aggregation is a parenthesis, or bar, which shows that the numbers enclosed by it are to be considered together, subject to the same operation; as, (5 × 6 ÷ 2) or 5 × 6 ÷ 2.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a rule? A problem? An axiom?
answer: A rule is a prescribed method of performing an operation. A problem is a question or proposition to be solved. An axiom is any truth which is self-evident.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is analysis in arithmetic?
answer: The process of investigating principles, and solving problems, independently of set rules.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is notation?
answer: Notation is a method of writing or expressing numbers by characters.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is numeration?
answer: Numeration is a method of reading numbers expressed by characters.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many systems of notation are in general use?
answer: Two: the Roman and the Arabic. Roman notation: V, IX, XC, DC, MDCVI, etc. Arabic notation: I, 35, 156, 1701, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Upon what is the Roman notation founded?
answer: Upon five principles: first, repeating a letter repeats its value; second, if a letter of any value is placed after one of greater value, it is added to the greater; third, if a letter of any value is placed before a greater, it is to be taken from the greater; fourth, if a letter of any value is placed between letters of higher value, it is to be taken from their united values; fifth, if a bar or dash is placed over a letter, it increases its value a thousandfold.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: In how many ways may numbers be written?
answer: In words, letters, and figures; as, five, V. 5.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: In the Arabic system of notation numbers are expressed according to what principle?
answer: According to the positions of the nine characters.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Why is the Arabic system of notation also called the decimal system?
answer: Because of its increase and decrease in a tenfold ratio, from decem, a Latin word meaning ten.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between the simple and local value of a figure?
answer: The simple value of a figure is its value when taken alone, while the local value depends upon the place it occupies with other figures.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the fundamental synthetic process of arithmetic?
answer: Addition, for by it we pass from unity to plurality; from the one to the many.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Give the principles of addition.
answer: Only similar numbers can be added. The sum is a number similar to the numbers added. The sum is the same in whatever order the numbers are added.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the cases in, or modes of addition?
answer: Mental addition and written addition.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Why do we write the numbers as suggested by the arith-methics, and why do we begin at the right hand to add?
answer: Merely for convenience.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the fundamental analytical process of arith-metic?
answer: Subtraction.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Give the principles of subtraction?
answer: Similar numbers, only, can be subtracted. The differ-ence is a number similar to the numbers subtracted. If the two numbers are equally increased or diminished the re-mainder will be the same. If the first number is equal to the sum of the second and the remainder, then the second must equal the difference between the first and the remainder.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name and define the cases in subtraction?
answer: One is expressed in the Arabic system, the other by some mental calculation.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name the principles of multiplication.
answer: The multiplier is always an abstract number. The product of two numbers is the same, whichever is made the multiplier. If the multiplicand be multiplied by all of the parts of the multiplier, the sum of all the partial products will be the true product. The multiplicand equals the quo-tient of the product divided by the multiplier; the multi-plier equals the quotient of the product divided by the multiplicand.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Why are the multiplicand and multiplier taken together called factors?
answer: Because they make the product.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the terms of division?
answer: The dividend, divisor and quotient.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Upon what does the quotient depend?
answer: Upon both dividend and divisor.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Show how the value of the quotient depends on the relation of dividend and divisor.
answer: If one of these terms is changed it changes the quotient, but if both are changed alike the changes neutralize each other and the quotient remains the same.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What principles of division are deduced from these relations?
answer: Multiplying the dividend or dividing the divisor by any number multiplies the quotient by that number. Dividing the dividend or multiplying the divisor by any number divides the quotient by that number. Multiplying or dividing both dividend and divisor by the same number does not change the quotient.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: In division, what relation exists in regard to the quality of abstractness and concreteness?
answer: The quotient may be either abstract or concrete. It will be abstract when the dividend and divisor are both abstract, or both concrete. It will be concrete when the dividend is concrete and the divisor abstract. Either the divisor or quotient must always be an abstract number. An abstract number cannot be divided by a concrete number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How are numbers classified?
answer: As even and odd; prime and composite; integral and fractional; abstract and concrete; simple and compound; like and unlike.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the minuend and subtrahend?
answer: The minuend is the number subtracted from; the subtrahend is the number subtracted.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a composite number?
answer: One that can be produced by multiplying two or more numbers together. Thus, 36 is a composite number because 3 X 12 will produce it, and 4 X 9 will, also.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is factoring?
answer: The process of separating numbers into factors.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the component factors of a number?
answer: The several numbers which, being multiplied together, produce the number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the powers of a number?
answer: The first power is the number itself, or the root. The second power, or square, is the number multiplied by itself. The third power, or cube, is the product arising from using the number three times as a factor.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the reciprocal of a number?
answer: It is 1 divided by that number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the true remainder by dividing by factors?
answer: Multiply each partial remainder, except the first, by all the preceding divisors, and add these products to the first remainder. It will be noticed that after the first division the remainder cannot be of the same denomination as the first number divided; and as each succeeding division increases the value of the remainders, each remainder must be multiplied by all the preceding divisors to reduce it to its former denomination.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a prime number? When are numbers prime to each other?
answer: One which cannot be resolved into factors. Numbers are prime to each other when they have no common divisor. 17 is a prime number and so is 29.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are a common divisor, and the greatest common divisor?
answer: One which will divide two or more numbers without a remainder. The greatest common divisor is the greatest number that will divide two or more numbers without a remainder.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are a multiple, a common multiple, and the least common multiple?
answer: A multiple is a number exactly divisible by a given number. A common multiple is one exactly divisible by two or more numbers. The least common multiple is the least number that is exactly divisible by two or more given numbers. Thus, 24 is a multiple of 4.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are fractions?
answer: Parts of a unit.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the value of a fraction?
answer: The quotient of the numerator divided by the denomi-nator.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What do the numerator and the denominator respect-ively show?
answer: The denominator shows into how many parts a number is divided; the numerator shows how many parts are taken.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are a proper fraction, an improper fraction, a mixed fraction and a complex fraction?
answer: A proper fraction is one whose numerator is smaller than its denominator. An improper fraction is one whose numerator is equal to or greater than its denominator. A mixed number is one expressed by an integer and fraction written together. A complex fraction is one that has a fraction in its numerator, or in its denominator, or in both.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Is a fraction a number?
answer: A fraction is a number of equal parts of unity, and consequently must represent a subdivision of numbers: thus, 5 feet is a number, and the fraction ⁵⁄₁₂ of a foot represents equal numbers of a lower order, as 5 inches.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Is a fraction a denominate number?
answer: No.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Why do we invert the divisor in division of fractions?
answer: For convenience; if it is not inverted the fractions must be reduced to a common denominator, and one numerator divided by the other.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the greatest common divisor of fractions?
answer: We find the greatest common divisor of the given numerators, and the least common multiple of the denominators.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the least common multiple of fractions?
answer: By finding the least common multiple of the numerators, and the greatest common divisor of the denominators.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are decimal fractions?
answer: Fractions whose denominators are increased or decreased in tenfold ratio.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the first thing to be considered in the treatment of decimals?
answer: The method of reading and writing them, or their numeration and notation.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What will the denominator of a decimal always be?
answer: The unit 1 with as many ciphers annexed as are equal to the number of figures in the decimal or numerator.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Upon what does the value of a decimal depend?
answer: Its distance from the decimal point.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do decimals increase and decrease?
answer: They increase from right to left, and decrease from left to right, in a tenfold ratio.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a pure decimal?
answer: A pure decimal consists of a decimal only; for example, .35, which is read, as if written, 35/100.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a mixed decimal?
answer: One with an integer and a decimal; thus, 15.7.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a complex decimal?
answer: One with a fraction annexed; thus, .005¾.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: From what are the names of decimal orders derived?
answer: From the names of the orders of integers.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the rule for the multiplication of decimals?
answer: Multiply as in simple numbers, and point off as many decimals in the product as the sum of the decimals in both multiplier and multiplicand. Examples: .12 3.02 .08 .04 .0096 .1208
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the rule for the division of decimals?
answer: Divide as in simple numbers, and point off as many decimals in the quotient as the decimal places of the dividend exceed those of the divisor. Examples: .12).132 .001).3482 1.1 348.2
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a circulating decimal?
answer: A decimal in which a figure or set of figures are continually repeating.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a repetend?
answer: The figure or set of figures continually repeated.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a continued fraction?
answer: A fraction whose numerator is 1, and whose denominatoris a whole number, plus a fraction whose numerator is also I, and whose denominator is a similar fraction, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between decimal fractions and common fractions?
answer: The decimal fraction has a denominator understood, showing that a unit is divided into ten equal parts, or subdivided in a tenfold ratio; the second has a denominator expressed, showing that a unit is divided into any number of equal parts.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is currency?
answer: The medium of circulation.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: From what was the sign $ derived?
answer: From the initial letters of the United States, U.S. joined together as a monogram.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an aliquot part of a number?
answer: Such a part as will exactly divide that number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a bill?
answer: A written statement of articles bought or sold, together with the price of each and the whole cost.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a compound number?
answer: A concrete number, whose value is expressed in two or more different denominations.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a denominate number?
answer: A concrete number in which the unit of measure is established by law or custom.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a simple denominate number?
answer: A denominate number composed of units of the same denomination; as, 3 feet.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a compound denominate number?
answer: One composed of two or more denominations which are related to each other; as, 3 feet 4 inches. Example: Reduce 5 yds., 2 ft., 3 in. to inches. --- --- --- --- Ans. 207 or 5 X 3 + 2 X 12 + 3 = 207 in., Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a standard unit?
answer: A unit of measure from which the other units of the same kind may be derived.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name the quantities of magnitude which give rise to denominate numbers?
answer: They are value, weight, length, surface, volume, capacity, angles, time.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of value?
answer: Money is the standard measure of value, and in the United States the standard unit is the dollar. In English money the pound sterling is the unit.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of weight?
answer: The Troy pound.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of length?
answer: The yard.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of surface?
answer: The square yard for ordinary measurement, and the acre for land.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of volume?
answer: The cubic yard for ordinary measurement, and the cord for wood. Example: Divide 45 A. of land among seven children so that each child will have an equal portion. Solution: 45 A. ÷ 7 = 6 A. and 3 A. remaining. 3 A. × 160 = 480 ÷ 7 = 68 sq. rds. and 4 sq. rds. remaining. 4 sq. rds. × 30¼ = 121 ÷ 7 = 17 sq. yds. and 2 sq. yds. remaining. 2 sq. yds. × 9 = 18 ÷ 7 = 2 sq. ft. and 4 sq. ft. remaining. 4 sq. ft. × 144 = 576 ÷ 7 = 82 sq. in. and 2 sq. in. remaining. Ans. 6 A., 68 sq. rds., 17 sq. yds., 2 sq. ft., 82⅔ sq. in. to each child.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of capacity?
answer: The gallon for fluids, and the bushel for dry measure.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of angles?
answer: The right angle.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the standard unit of time?
answer: The day. This is determined by the revolution of the earth.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a measure?
answer: That by which extent, dimension, capacity, or amount is ascertained. Measures are of eight kinds: length, surface or area, solidity, weight, time, angles, capacity and money.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many dimensions has extension?
answer: Extension has three: length, breadth, and thickness.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Explain how distance is measured by time?
answer: Every circle is supposed to be divided into 360 equal parts called degrees. As the earth makes its revolution once in 24 hours, the 24 hours must equal 360°, or 1 hour, 15° of distance. By subdividing, we find that 1° would equal 4 minutes, and 1/15 of a degree would equal 4 seconds.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the difference in time corresponding to any given difference in longitude?
answer: Divide the difference in longitude, expressed in degrees, minutes and seconds, by 15, and the respective quotients will be hours, minutes, and seconds.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the difference in longitude corresponding to any given difference in time?
answer: Multiply the difference in time, expressed in hours, minutes and seconds, by 15, and its respective products will be degrees, minutes and seconds of longitude.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the time at one place, when the time at another place and the difference of these places in time are known?
answer: When the second place is east of the first, add their difference of time; when it is west of the first, subtract their difference of time.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do navigators determine their longitude at sea?
answer: Taking with them a chronometer set to mark the time at a given place — as Greenwich or Washington — they ascertain by observation of the sun with the sextant the time at the place they are in, reduce the difference of time to the difference of longitude, and thus ascertain that they are so many degrees east or west of the meridian of the place for which the chronometer is set.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a scale?
answer: A series of numbers, descending or ascending, used in operations upon compound numbers.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is weight?
answer: A measure of the quantity of matter a body contains, according to some fixed standard.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Describe Gunter's chain.
answer: It consists of 100 links, each link containing 7.92 inches, or the whole length 66 feet, or 4 rods.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between a pound Troy and a pound avoirdupois?
answer: The Troy pound has 5,760 grains: the avoirdupois, 7,000.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is linear measure?
answer: That which is used in measuring lines or distances.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are a square, and a cube?
answer: A square is a superficial figure having four equal sides and four equal angles; a cube is a solid figure having six equal sides.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many inches in a wine gallon, in a beer gallon, and in a bushel?
answer: In a wine gallon, 231 cubic inches; in a beer gallon, 282; in a bushel, 2,150.4 cubic inches.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are duodecimals?
answer: The division and subdivision of a unit, resulting by continually dividing by 12.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is percentage? What is its base?
answer: Such part of a number as is represented by the per cent. Its base is the number on which the percentage is reckoned.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What do the words per cent, mean?
answer: By the hundred.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name and define the elements involved in percentage?
answer: The elements are: the base, or number of which the per cent. is taken; the rate, or number of hundredths taken; the percentage, or quantity taken; the amount, or sum of the base and the percentage; and the difference, or base less the percentage.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between rate and rate per cent?
answer: The rate is the number of hundredths, and the rate per cent is the fraction which denotes how many hundredths are taken. Thus, in 3%, or 3/100, 3 is the rate and 3/100 the rate per cent.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Give the applications of percentage.
answer: The rules of percentage are applied to the computing of interest, discount, commission, taxes, insurance, duties, and especially profit and loss.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is commission? What is brokerage?
answer: The percentage allowed an agent, factor or commission merchant, is commission. Brokerage is the fee paid to a dealer in money, stocks, etc., for the transaction of business. The reader should always bear in mind that 50% of a number would be ½ of it; 33⅓% of a number is ⅓ of it; and 20% of anything is ⅕ of it; 5% of a number is ¹⁄₂₀ of it; while 2% of anything is ¹⁄₅₀ of it. Example 1. Find 9% of $284. Solution: $284 × .09 = $25.56, Ans. Example 2. $21.96 is 6% of what number? Solution: $21.96 ÷ .06 = $366, Ans. Example 3. $430 is 7½% more than what number? Solution: That number must be 100% of itself. Then 100% + 7½% must = $430. $430 ÷ 107½ = $4, which is 1% of the number, and 100% = 100 × $4, or $400, Ans. Example 4. A man bought 50 shares of stock at 96½ and sold it at par. Allowing ½c. brokerage on each transaction, how much did he gain on the 50 shares, provided the shares were $10 each? Solution: One dollar's worth of stock would cost him 96½c. + ½c. or 97c.; and a share would cost him 97c. X 10 or $9.70; and 50 shares would cost him $9.70 X 50 or $485. Now he sells one dollar's worth for 100c. — ¼c. brokerage, or 99¾c., and a ten-dollar share would bring him 99¾c. X 10, or $9.98¾; and 50 shares would bring him $9.98¾ X 50, or $499.37½. And $499.37½ — $485 = $14.37½, Ans. Example 5. A man had $3875 to invest in stock, which was quoted in the market at 96½; if he had to pay also ¼c. commission, how many one-hundred-dollar shares could he buy? Solution: One dollar's worth of stock would cost him 96½c. + ¼c., or 96¾c.; and with his $3875 he could purchase as many dollars' worth of stock as .96¾ is contained in $3875, or $4015.54; and there would be as many shares as $100 is contained in $4015.54, or 40 shares, with $15.54 still uninvested. Example 6. If a company declares a dividend on its stock of 4½ per cent. and this dividend amounts in cash to $4050, what is the amount of the stock of that company? Solution: If one dollar of stock yields 4½c., then to yield $4050 it would take as many dollars of stock as 4½c. is contained in $4050, or $90,000 worth of stock. $4050 ÷ .04½ = $90,000, Ans. Example 7. A man bought 250 one-hundred-dollar shares of stock at 99 per cent., and sold 100 shares at 101 per cent., and 150 shares at 98 per cent. If he paid ⅛c. brokerage for the buying, and ⅛c. brokerage for the selling of all of it, did he gain or lose in the transaction, and how much? Solution: 99c. + ⅛c. = 99⅛c. X 100 = $99.12½, cost of one share. $99.12½ X 250 = $24,781.25, cost of entire stock. 1.01 — ⅛c. = 1.00⅞ X 100 = $100.87½, selling price of one share. $100.87½ X 100 = $10,087.50, selling price of one hundred shares. .98 - ¼c. = .97¾ X 100 = $97.87½, selling price of one share. $97.87½ X 150 = $14,681.25, selling price of one hundred and fifty shares. $24,781.25 cost — ($10,087.50 + $14,681.25) = $12.50 loss, Ans. Example 8. A company has a capital stock of $75,000, and loses $1,875 the first year in business. What per cent. of assessment must be made on the stockholders to make up this deficit? Solution: If $75,000 must make up a loss of $1,875, then one dollar of the stock would be assessed ⁷⁵⁰⁰⁰ of $1,875, or 2½ per cent., Ans. 75000)1875.000(.025 150000 375000 375000
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a commission merchant?
answer: An agent who buys and sells goods for another on commission.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are a consignee, and a consignor?
answer: A person who receives goods to sell for another is a consignee; a consignor is a person who sends goods to another to be sold.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a company? What is a corporation?
answer: A company is a group of individuals united for the purpose of performing some business or undertaking. A corporation is a company formed and authorized by law to act as one person.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a share of stock, and what is a stockholder?
answer: Stock is the amount of capital invested, and a share is one of the equal parts into which capital stock is divided. A stockholder is the owner of stock.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between a charter and a firm?
answer: A charter defines the powers of an incorporated body, while a firm is the name under which an unincorporated body transacts business.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is stock at par?
answer: Stock is at par when it sells for its face value; it is said to be above par when it sells for more, and below par when it sells for less.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is profit and loss?
answer: A commercial term, used to express the gain or loss in business transactions.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the loss or gain per cent. when the cost and selling price are given?
answer: Divide the gain or loss by the purchase price, or cost.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the selling price when the cost and gain or loss per cent. are given?
answer: Multiply the purchase price by $1, increased or diminished, as the case may be, by the gain or loss per cent.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the cost when the selling price and the gain or loss per cent. are given?
answer: Divide the selling price by $1, increased or diminished, as the case may be, by the gain or loss per cent. Example 1. How should goods be marked, that cost $25, so there will be a gain of 12% when sold? Solution: 12% of $25 is $3, and $25 + $3 = $28, Ans. Example 2. Goods were marked to sell for $59, which was at a profit of 18%. What was the cost of the goods? Solution: The goods cost 100%, and if they sold at a profit of 18%, then $59 is 118% of the cost; and $59 ÷ 118 = .50 or 1%; and 100% = .50 × 100, or $50, cost of goods, Ans. Example 3. A house was sold for $2,600 that cost the owner $2,925. What was his loss per cent.? Solution: $2925 - $2600 = $325, the loss in money; and if $2925, the cost, lost $325, then one dollar of the cost would lose 325/2925 of $325, or 11 1/9%, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an instalment? An assessment? A dividend?
answer: An instalment is an advance payment required of the stockholders on their subscriptions to the capital stock. An assessment is a sum required of the stockholders to meet the losses of the business expenses of the company. A dividend is a sum paid to the stockholders from the profits of the business.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is insurance? A policy? A premium?
answer: Insurance is security on property guaranteed by one party to another for a stipulated sum, against the loss of property by any casualty. A policy is a written contract between the parties. A premium is the sum periodically paid for insurance.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define an underwriter.
answer: An underwriter is an insurer—the person who takes the risk.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name the kinds of insurance.
answer: There are six different kinds of insurance: Fire insurance, Marine insurance, Cyclone insurance, Accident insurance, Health insurance, Life insurance.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a tax? A poll tax? An assessor?
answer: A tax is a money payment assessed on the person or on property of the individual for public purposes. A poll tax is a head-tax—a sum required of each male citizen liable to Taxation, without regard to his property. An assessor is the person appointed to prepare the assessment-roll and apportion the taxes.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an inventory?
answer: A written list of articles of property, with their values.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are general average and jetsam?
answer: General average is a method of ascertaining the loss to be sustained by the proprietors of ships, freight and cargo respectively where a part of the property has been sacrificed or damaged for the common safety. Jetsam is the part of the goods thrown overboard.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are duties or customs, and what is a custom-house?
answer: Duties or customs are taxes levied on imported goods. A custom-house is an office established by the government for the transaction of business relating to duties.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is smuggling?
answer: Carrying on foreign commerce secretly, without paying the duties imposed by law.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many kinds of duties are there?
answer: Define them. There are two kinds of duties: ad valorem and specific. An ad valorem duty is the sum computed on the cost of the goods in the country from which they were imported. A specific duty is a sum computed on the weight or measure of the goods, without regard to their cost.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is interest, and what is usury?
answer: A sum paid for the use of money. Usury is interest above the legal rate.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a custom tariff?
answer: A list of the rates of duties to be collected.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between simple and compound interest?
answer: Simple interest is the interest on the principal only; compound interest is the interest on the principal and the previous interest added.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The time, rate per cent, and interest being given, how do we find the principal?
answer: Divide the given interest by the interest on $1 for the given time at the given rate. Example: What is the face of a note that in 1 yr. 6 mo. 15 da. at 6% would yield $12.95 interest? Solution: One dollar at 6% for 1 yr. 6 mo. 15 da. would yield 9¼ cents, and to yield $12.95 it would take as many dollars as 9¼ cents is contained in $12.95, or $140, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The time, rate per cent, and amount being given, how do we find the principal?
answer: Divide the given amount by the amount of $1 for the given time at the given rate. Example: A certain note in 2 yr. 3 mo. and 10 da. at 8% amounted (principal and interest) to $127.68. What was the face of the note? Solution: One dollar at 8% for 2 yr. 3 mo. and 10 da. would yield 18.2⅔ cents, and the amount of the one dollar would be $1.182⅔, and it would take as many dollars to amount to $127.68 as $1.182⅔ is contained in $127.68, or $108, face of note, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The principal, time and interest being given, how do we find the rate per cent.?
answer: Divide the given interest by the interest on the principal at 1 per cent, for the time. Example: A note of $246 in 2 yr. 6 mo. yielded $27.67¾ interest. What was the rate per cent.? Solution: At one per cent, the $246 for 2 yr. 6 mo. would yield $6.15; and for the note to yield $27.675 it would take as many times one per cent. as $6.15 is contained in $27,675, or 4½%, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The principal, interest, and rate per cent. being given, how do we find the time?
answer: Divide the given interest by the interest on the principal for one year at the rate per cent.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a partial payment? An indorsement?
answer: Partial payment of a note, bond, or other obligation is the payment of a certain amount of the money due on the obligation. An indorsement is anything written on the back.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is discount? What is the present worth of a debt, and how do we find it?
answer: Discount is an allowance made for the payment of a debt before it is due. The present worth of a debt is such a sum as, being put at legal interest, would amount to the debt when due. It is found by dividing the given debt by the amount of $1 at interest for the given time and rate.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a bank? What is bank discount? What are days of grace?
answer: A bank is a corporation chartered by law for the purpose of receiving and loaning money, and perhaps also of issuing a paper circulation. Bank discount is an allowance made to a bank for the payment of a debt before it is due. Days of grace are three days allowed a debtor to pay his obligations after they become due. This practice has been discontinued in many of the States. Example I. A man desiring some ready money made out and signed a note for $275, due in 6 mo. He took it to a bank, and the bank discounted it at 8% and handed him the proceeds in cash. How much money did he get? Solution: 8% on $275 for 6 mo. is $11, which the bank demanded; and they gave the man $275 — $11, or $264, Ans. (At the end of the 6 mo. the man would have to return to the bank and pay the bank $275 cash, and take up his note.) Example 2. A party took a note to a bank and had it discounted at 6% for 3 mo. The banker handed the party in cash $65.01. How large was the note? Solution: One dollar discounted at 6% for 3 mo. would yield 98½c.; and to yield $65.01 it would take as great a note as 98½c. is contained in $65.01, or $66, the size of the note, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a promissory note? What is a bank-note? What is a negotiable note?
answer: A promissory note is a written agreement to pay a certain sum either on demand or at a specified time. Bank-notes are paper currency issued by banks as money. A negotiable note is one which may be bought and sold, or negotiated, and is made payable to the bearer or to the order of the payee.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name and define the kinds of notes.
answer: They are classified as time notes, negotiable notes, joint notes, bank-notes, drafts, stock, and bonds.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a notary public? What is a protest?
answer: An officer authorized by law to attest the validity of documents or undertakings of any kind. A protest is a formal declaration in writing, made by a notary public, at the request of the holder of a note, notifying the makers and the indorsers of its non-payment.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a draft? A check?
answer: A draft is a note of exchange, issued by one party, calling upon a second party to pay over to a third party so much money, and charge therewith the account of the third party. A check is also a note of exchange, but made payable at some bank where the person issuing it has de-posits or credit.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a bond? What is a coupon?
answer: Bonds are interest-bearing notes issued by nations, states, cities, railroad companies, and other corporations as a means of borrowing money. The coupons are the due-bills for the interest, which, as they become due, are cut off and presented for payment.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What do the terms "five-twenties" and "ten-forties" mean?
answer: They are applied to government bonds and indicate the time when due. The five-twenties are due twenty years after issue, although the government can pay them any time after five if they choose to do so. The same rule of interpretation applies to the ten-forties. These war-bonds are now out of date.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is exchange, and how many kinds are there?
answer: Exchange is a method of remitting money from one per-son to another, or making payments by written orders. There are two kinds, domestic and foreign.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define these.
answer: Domestic or inland exchange is the exchange between different places in the same country. Foreign exchange is the remittance made between different countries.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a bill of exchange?
answer: It is a written request or order upon one person to pay a certain sum to another person, or to his order, at a speci-fied time. A draft or check is one kind of bill of exchange.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many parties are there to a transaction in ex-change?
answer: There are always three, and usually four.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a set of exchange? What is a course of exchange?
answer: It consists of three copies of the same bill sent by different conveyances to provide against a loss; when one has been paid the others are void. A course of exchange is the current price paid in one place for bills of exchange on another place. Example 1. A drover went to a bank in New York with $2450, and desired a draft to mail to his son in Kansas City. How large a draft could he get if the bank charged ⅓ of one per cent. for writing up and giving the draft to him? Solution: A dollar draft would cost the drover $1 plus the ⅓c. that the bank charged. Hence a dollar draft cost $1.00⅓, and he could get as large a draft for his money as $1.00⅓ is contained in the $2450, or $2437.81, Ans. Example 2. A lady went to a bank in Pittsburg one day and purchased a draft to send to her daughter in Atlanta. The bank charged her 1¼ per cent. exchange. She paid for the draft but did not have money enough to pay the exchange. So the bank waited until the following Saturday, when she paid them $3.75 in full. How large a draft did the lady send to her daughter the day previous? Solution: If the bank charged .01¼c. for writing up a draft for one dollar, then if the lady paid exchange to the amount of $3.75 her draft must have been as large as .01¼c. is contained in $3.75, or $300, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is equation of payments?
answer: The process of finding the mean time of the payment of several sums, due at different times, without interest.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is equated time?
answer: The date at which several debts may be paid at one time.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the focal date?
answer: The date by which all others are compared in averaging an account.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is partnership?
answer: A relation established by two or more persons in trade. The individuals thus associated are called partners.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between simple and compound partnership?
answer: A simple partnership is one in which each of the partners has his capital invested for the same length of time. A compound partnership is that in which the capital of the partners is employed for different periods of time.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an indorsement?
answer: The indorsement of a bill of exchange is the writing upon its back by which the payee relinquishes his title, and transfers the payment to another.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an acceptance?
answer: The acceptance of a bill is the promise which the drawee makes to pay it at maturity.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How is this obligation acknowledged?
answer: By writing the word "accepted," with his signature, across the face of the bill.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a bankrupt?
answer: A bankrupt is a person who fails in business and has not property enough to pay his bills.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between a bankrupt and an insolvent?
answer: "Bankrupt" is strictly a term applicable only to a trader, while the term "insolvent" applies to any person who is unable to pay his debts.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define bankruptcy.
answer: It is the condition of the business of a bankrupt.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is analysis?
answer: It is the process of arriving at a required result by following a course of reasoning, and not by formal rules.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is ratio?
answer: Define each kind. Ratio is the comparison of two numbers with each other. Arithmetical ratio is the difference between two numbers. Geometrical ratio is one divided by the other.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the terms of a ratio?
answer: The two numbers compared.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are the antecedent and consequent?
answer: The antecedent is the first term of a ratio; the consequent is the second term.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What are direct and inverse ratio?
answer: Direct ratio is dividing the consequent by the antecedent. Inverse ratio is dividing the antecedent by the consequent.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: In how many ways may the ratio of two numbers be expressed?
answer: In two ways: By placing a colon between the terms, or by the form of a fraction, taking the antecedent for the numerator and the consequent for the denominator. Example: What is the ratio between 6 and 10? 6 : 10 or 6/10, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the difference between a simple ratio and a compound ratio?
answer: A simple ratio is the ratio of two numbers. A compound ratio is the product of two or more simple ratios. Example 1. Is the ratio the same between 7 and 21 as between 15 and 45? Solution: 7 : 21 :: 15 : 45 7 15 — = — 21 45 1 1 — = — Ans., Yes. 3 3 Example 2. What number has the same ratio to 65 that 5 has to 15? Solution: 5 : 15 :: what : 65 5 what — = — 15 65 1 21⅔ — = —, Ans. 3 65
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is proportion?
answer: An equality of ratios.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Which are the extremes and the means of a stated proportion?
answer: The extremes are the first and fourth terms of a stated proportion. The means are the other two.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a continued proportion?
answer: A proportion with more than two equal ratios; as, 3:5::6:10::9:15.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many antecedents are there in a proportion, and how many consequents?
answer: As each ratio has an antecedent and consequent, the first and third terms of a proportion are the antecedents, and the second and fourth the consequents.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How are ratio and proportion distinguished?
answer: Ratio is the relation between two numbers, while proportion is the relation between two ratios?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a simple proportion?
answer: An equality between two simple ratios. Example: In a sack of 162 lb. of rough rice the ratio of hulls to clean rice is as 1 to 4. How many pounds of clean rice in the sack? Solution: 1 : 5 :: what : 162 1/5 = what/162 1/5 = 32⅖/162 hulls hulls 1 : 5 :: 32⅖ : 162 162 — 32⅖ = 129⅗, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a direct proportion?
answer: One in which each term increases or diminishes, as the one on which it depends increases or diminishes.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an inverse proportion?
answer: One in which each term increases as the term upon which it depends diminishes, or diminishes as it increases.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a compound proportion?
answer: A proportion in which either ratio is compound. Example: Two planters expended $2240 on an irrigating plant. One owned 80 A. and the other 120 A. If they paid in the ratio of their acreage, how much did each pay? Solution: 80 : 200 :: what : 2240 80   what --- = ---- 200  2240 2   what --- = ---- 5   2240 2   896 --- = ---- 5   2240 80 : 200 :: 896 : 2240 Hence the 80 A. pays $896. And also 120 : 200 :: what : 2240 120  what ---- = ---- 200  2240 3   what --- = ---- 5   2240 3   1344 --- = ---- 5   2240 120 : 200 :: 1344 : 2240 Hence the 120 A. pays $1344.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Of what does alligation treat?
answer: Of mixing or compounding two or more ingredients of different values.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How many kinds of alligation are there?
answer: Define each. Two: medial and alternate. Medial is the process of finding the average price or quality of several ingredients whose prices or qualities are known. Alternate is the process of finding the proportional quantity to be taken of several ingredients whose prices are known.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Give the analysis of alligation.
answer: Alligation is but a short way of performing an analysis. To show the process we will unite 3, 4, 7, 10, to form a value worth 6. It will be noticed that the difference between 10 and 6 is a loss of 4, and to make a loss of 1 will require ¼, also there will be a gain of ⅓ in the opposite connection. The proportion, then, if the two terms 3 and 10, expressed in fractions, is ⅓ and ¼, which, reduced to a common denominator, is ⁴⁄₁₂ and ³⁄₁₂, or expressed in whole numbers, 4 and 3, which, in alligation, is understood as an exchange of difference without the use of fractions and their reduction to whole numbers.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an annuity?
answer: A sum of money payable at certain intervals of time, usually one year.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Name and define the kinds of annuity.
answer: A certain annuity, which continues for a fixed period of time; a perpetual annuity, continuing forever; a contingent annuity, depending upon certain occurrences, as on the death of a person; an annuity in reversion, one that begins at a specified future time or event; and an annuity in arrears, one the payments of which have been allowed to accumulate instead of being paid. Examples: A man by will left to his son a stipend of $10 per month until the son reached the age of 30 years. This would be a certain annuity. Where a person settles upon another a sum of money, say $50 per year to continue till the said person contracts marriage, this would be a contingent annuity. If a person contracts to pay to another person a sum of money, say $5 per month not to commence till the person reaches the age of 50 years, and then continue the rest of his life, this would be an annuity in reversion. Where a person grants to another a sum of money, say $8 per month to commence at birth and none of the money to be paid over till the beneficiary reaches the age of 21 years, this would be an annuity in arrears.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is involution?
answer: The process of raising a number to a given power.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a surd?
answer: The indicated root of an imperfect power.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the power of a number?
answer: A power of a number is the result obtained by using it a certain number of times as a factor.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a perfect power? An imperfect power?
answer: A perfect power is a number whose root can be found. An imperfect power is one whose root cannot be exactly found.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the exponent of a power?
answer: The exponent of a power is a number placed at the right of the root and just above it, to show the number of times the root is to be used, as 3⁴ = 3 × 3 × 3 × 3.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define evolution.
answer: Evolution is the process of extracting the root of any number considered a power. It is the opposite of involution.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the root of a number?
answer: The root of a number is one of the equal factors of that number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How are the roots of numbers indicated?
answer: By the sign called a radical √. The figure on the sign indicates the root. Examples: √64 = 8 ³√27 = 3 ⁴√16 = 2 ⁵√243 = 3
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the square root of a number?
answer: One of two equal factors that produce that number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the cube root of a number?
answer: One of three equal factors that produce that number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is an arithmetical progression?
answer: A series of numbers increased or diminished by a common difference. Examples: Common difference, 3. Then 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, etc., would be an arithmetical progression.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a geometrical progression?
answer: A series of numbers increased or diminished by a constant multiplier. Common multiplier, 4. Then 2, 8, 32, 128, 512, etc., would be a geometrical progression.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What five things are to be considered in an arithmetical progression?
answer: The first term, the last term, the number of terms, the common difference and the sum of the series. Three of these being given, the other two can be found.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What things are to be considered in a geometrical progression?
answer: They are the first term, the last term, the number of terms, the constant multiplier, and the sum of the series. Three of these being given, the other two can be found.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is a duodecimal?
answer: A denominate number in which twelve units of any denomination make a unit of the next higher denomination.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is mensuration?
answer: The art of measuring magnitudes.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is magnitude?
answer: Anything which has length, breadth and thickness.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the measuring unit of surfaces?
answer: It is customary to use the square as the measuring unit, as a square inch, foot, rod, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the area of a square?
answer: By multiplying the length by the breadth.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the area of (a) a rhombus, (b) of a trapezium, (c) of a triangle?
answer: (a) By multiplying the length by the altitude; (b) by multiplying half the sum of the parallel sides by the altitude; (c) by multiplying the base by half the altitude.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the area of a triangle when the three sides are given?
answer: From half the sum of the three sides subtract each side respectively; then multiply together half the sum and the three remainders, and extract the square root of the product. Example: The three sides of a triangle are 5 rods, 9 rods and 12 rods: to find the area. Solution: (5 + 9 + 12) ÷ 2 = 13 13 - 5 = 8 13 - 9 = 4 13 - 12 = 1 8 X 4 X 1 X 13 = 416 √416 = 20.+, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the circumference of a circle when the diameter is given?
answer: To find the circumference multiply the diameter by 3.14159; to find the diameter, divide the circumference by the same number.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the area of a circle?
answer: By multiplying half the circumference by half the diameter; or, by multiplying the circumference by a fourth of the diameter.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the side of the greatest square that can be inscribed in a circle of a given diameter?
answer: Divide the square of the given diameter by 2, and extract the square root of the quotient. Example: What is the side of the greatest square that can be inscribed within a circle whose diameter is 16 ft.? 16² = 256 ÷ 2 = 128 √128 = 11.3+, Ans.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find a mean proportional between two numbers?
answer: By multiplying the given numbers together, and extracting the square root of the product.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the solidity of bodies whose sides are perpendicular to each other?
answer: By multiplying the length, breadth and thickness.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the solidity of a prism?
answer: By multiplying the area of the base by the height.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the lateral surface of a right prism?
answer: By multiplying the length by the perimeter of the base.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the solidity of a pyramid or cone?
answer: By multiplying the area of the base by ⅓ of the altitude.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the lateral or convex surface of a regular pyramid or cone?
answer: By multiplying the perimeter of the base by ½ of the slant height.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the solidity of a cylinder?
answer: By multiplying the area of the base by the height or length.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the convex surface of a cylinder?
answer: By multiplying the circumference of the base by the height.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the surface of a sphere or globe?
answer: By multiplying the circumference by the diameter.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the solidity of a sphere or globe?
answer: By multiplying the surface by ⅓ of the diameter.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do you find the side of a square equal in area to any given surface?
answer: By extracting the square root of the given surface.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we measure lumber?
answer: Multiply the length in feet by the product of the breadth and thickness, both in inches, and divide the result by 12.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the solid contents of a stick of round timber?
answer: By multiplying the length by ¼ the mean circumference.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is gauging?
answer: The process of finding the contents or capacity of casks, barrels, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How do we find the contents of casks?
answer: Multiply the square of the mean diameter by the length, and this product by .0034 to obtain the wine gallons. Using the decimal .0028 will give us the beer gallons.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Define the metric system of measurement.
answer: The metric system is a decimal system of weights and measures, having the metre for the base or unit.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is the measure of the metre in inches?
answer: It measures 39.3708 inches.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: From what was the metre obtained?
answer: It is one ten-millionth part of a quadrant of the earth's meridian. Illustration: A meridian passing around the earth and through each pole is about 24,750 miles long, and ¼ of it would be 6187.5 miles. Reduced to feet by multiplying by 5,280, this product divided by 10,000,000 would give 39.37 inches, the length of a metre.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: One gentleman meeting another, and inquiring the time past 12 o'clock, received for an answer: ⅓ of the time from now to midnight; what o'clock in the afternoon was it?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Where shall a pole 60 feet high be broken, that the top may rest on the ground 20 feet from the stump?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: Suppose a man owes $1,000. What sum shall he pay yearly so as to cancel the debt, principal and interest, at the end of three years, reckoning it at 6 per cent. simple interest?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A gentleman has a garden 400 feet long and 300 feet wide which he would raise 9 inches higher by means of the earth to be dug out of a ditch that goes around it. To what depth must the ditch be dug, supposing its breadth to be everywhere 6 feet?
answer: 10 1/78. 5. Divide $1,000 among A, B and C, so that A may have $156 more than B, and B $62 less than C. Ans. A, $416 2/3; B, $260 2/3; C, $322 2/3.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: If 21 cows eat up 8 acres of grass in 6 weeks, and 18 cows eat up the same in 9 weeks, how many cows will it maintain for 18 weeks, if the grass grow uniformly during that time?
answer: 15 cows. 7. Required the thickness of the lead of a pipe 1 1/4 inch bore, which weighs 16 lbs., a yard in length, a cubic foot of lead weighing 11,325 oz. avoirdupois. Ans. 2.329+ in. 8. If a cubic foot of brass were drawn into wire 1/10 of an inch in diameter, required the length of the wire, supposing no loss in the metal. Ans. 55 m., 4 fur., 104 yds., 2 ft., 4 in.+. 9. Divide $25 between 2 boys in such a proportion that, after it is divided, A shall have 1/2 more than B. Ans. A, $15; B, $10.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A makes B a present of $100, on condition that he shall expend it in cows, sheep, and geese; cows at $10 each, sheep at $1, and geese at 16 2/3 cents, so as to have just 100 in the whole. How many must he purchase of each?
answer: 5 cows, 41 sheep, and 54 geese.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: If 1/2 of 6 be 3, what will 1/4 of 20 be?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: How long must I keep $300 to balance the use of $500, which I lent a friend 4 months?
answer: 6 2/3 months. 13. If 800 men have provisions for 2 months, how many must leave that the remainder may subsist 5 months on the same? Ans. 480.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A hare, running 36 rods a minute, has 57 rods the start of a dog; how far must the dog run to overtake him, running 40 rods per minute?
answer: 570 rods.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The hour and minute hands of a watch are together at 12 o'clock; when are they next together?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: If a pole be ⅓ in the mud, ⅖ in the water, and 6 feet out of the water, what is its length?
answer: 90 feet.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A man being asked how many geese he had, replied: If I had ⅖ as many as I now have, and 2½ geese more, I should have 100; how many had he?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: The head of a fish is 4 feet long, the tail as long as the head and ⅓ the length of the body, the body as long as the head and tail; what is the length of the fish?
answer: 32 feet.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A and B can build a wall in 4 days, B and C in 6 days, A and C in 5 days; required the time if they work together.
answer: 3 3/7 days.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A man left his two sons, one 14, the other 18 years old, $1,000, so divided that their shares, being put at 6 per cent. interest, should be equal when each should be 21 years old; what was the share of each?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: A gentleman divided his fortune among his sons, giving A $9 as often as B $5, and C $3 as often as B $7: C received $7,442.10¾; what was the whole estate?
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON WRITTEN ARITHMETIC

question: What is bookkeeping?
answer: Bookkeeping is the art of recording business transactions in a systematic manner to show the true condition of a business. It is the science of accounts.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is a business transaction?
answer: A business transaction is, strictly, an exchange of equal values, or regarded as such.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is a debtor?
answer: A debtor is one who receives values.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is a creditor?
answer: A creditor is one from whom value is received.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: State what an account is.
answer: An account is a collection of all debits and credits involved in any business transaction, arranged under some title or heading.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Define debits and credits.
answer: Debits are entries against debtors. Credits are entries in favor of creditors.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is the proper title for an account?
answer: It is the name by which the person or thing is known. Day Book Lowell, Mass., Oct. 1, 1897. section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: How many and what sides has an account?
answer: Two sides, the left hand or debit side, and the right hand or credit side.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What does each side contain?
answer: The debit side contains the sums for which the account has become indebted to the business. The credit side contains the sums for which the business has become indebted to the account.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What does each side show?
answer: The debit side shows resources and losses. The credit side shows liabilities and gains.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What are resources, assets, or effects?
answer: Property belonging to a person, firm or corporation is thus termed according to certain conditions.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What are liabilities?
answer: All the debts and obligations of a business or corporation.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is the balance of an account?
answer: The difference between the two sides.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: If the balance is on the debit side, what is shown?
answer: Either a resource or a loss.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: If the balance is on the credit side, what is shown?
answer: Either a liability or a gain.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: How are accounts classified?
answer: As real or financial, representative or business.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Define real accounts.
answer: Real accounts are those which represent resources, or liabilities, such as cash, notes receivable, notes payable, and personal accounts.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Define representative accounts.
answer: Representative accounts are those that represent gains or losses, as merchandise, real estate, etc.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What two systems of bookkeeping are there?
answer: Single entry and double entry.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What advantage has one over the other?
answer: The main distinction is that while in single entry a record is kept of resources and liabilities, in double entry, a similar and additional record is kept of gains and losses. In single entry an account is kept with but one party to the transaction, either the debtor or creditor. In double entry an account is kept with both parties.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What books are necessary in single entry?
answer: Day book, journal, cash book, and ledger.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: For what is the day book used?
answer: To record business transactions as they occur.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What particulars of a business transaction should be recorded?
answer: The date, the transaction, the name of the person, firm or corporation, nature of payment, items with price.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Illustrate.
answer: June 1, 1898, Bought of John Arms, for my note at 60 days, 100 bbls. flour @ $5.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What will be shown at any time by a cash book properly balanced?
answer: The amount of cash on hand.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: How often, in practice, should the cash balance be verified?
answer: At the close of each business day.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: If the cash book is correctly kept, how will the debit and credit sides compare?
answer: The debit side will usually exceed the credit side. Ledger 1 SMITH, BROWN & CO. section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Give particulars as to use of ledger.
answer: The ledger is the final book of entry. It contains a record of all the debits and credits in every business trans-action. These debits and credits are arranged under appropriate heads, and give, in a condensed form, a state-ment of financial standing.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: Give abbreviations used in bookkeeping for the follow-ing terms: at, account, debtor, creditor, merchandise, the same.
answer: @, a/c, Dr., Cr., Mdse., Do. or ditto.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: How is an account closed?
answer: An account is closed when its debtor and creditor sides are made equal in amount.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING

question: What is the object of closing an account?
answer: To keep it from becoming too long, or too large, and also, when there is a balance, to separate it from the rest of the account, so that when it is closed, and the balance brought down, the account may begin again with but one sum.
section: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS ON BOOKKEEPING
